name: Webvan Group, Inc.
description: Webvan Group, Inc. was a prominent American dot-com company that operated an online grocery delivery service. Founded in 1996 and headquartered in Foster City, California, the company aimed to revolutionize retail by integrating a proprietary webstore with automated distribution facilities and a dedicated delivery fleet. Despite significant initial investment and rapid expansion, the company struggled with high operational costs and logistical challenges. Webvan ultimately ceased all operations and filed for bankruptcy in 2001, becoming a widely cited example of the risks associated with the late 1990s dot-com bubble.
